authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 117 is the primary public regulatory baseline for beverage facilities subject to FDA human-food rules, including cGMP, recall-plan, corrective-action, verification, and supply-chain expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: line readiness, in-process control, corrective-action, supplier-program, and completion-boundary review
used_for: authoritative beverage execution and records posture

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S is the primary federal source for traceability lot codes, critical tracking events, and key data elements when covered beverage ingredients or products fall under the FDA traceability rule.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ingredient receipt, lot assignment, transformation, traceability reconciliation, and hold or release review

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 120 - Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Point (HACCP) Systems
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 120 is the controlling public rule for juice sold as such or used as an ingredient in beverages and defines HACCP, sanitation SOP, corrective-action, verification, and records expectations for juice lines.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: juice beverage sanitation, HACCP checkpoint handling, corrective actions, and record review

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 129 - Processing and Bottling of Bottled Drinking Water
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 129 is the controlling public rule for bottled drinking water processing and bottling, including approved source, sanitary operations, lot, holding, and shipping expectations for bottled-water lines.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: source approval, water handling, bottling-room readiness, lot control, and shipment preparation

authoritative_sources.title: Bottled Water/Carbonated Soft Drinks Guidance Documents & Regulatory Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's beverage-topic index is the official public gateway tying bottled-water and carbonated-soft-drink workflow questions back to the applicable CFR and regulatory references used by beverage operators.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: product-family applicability and regulatory branch selection
used_for: avoidance of unsupported one-size-fits-all beverage assumptions

authoritative_sources.title: Initiation of Voluntary Recalls Under 21 CFR Part 7, Subpart C
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA recall guidance is an authoritative operating reference for when beverage inventory, lots, or shipments must stop moving through normal execution and shift into hold, recall, or formal quality-led disposition.
used_for: refusal to continue execution under recall conditions

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized standards body for identifier and traceability exchange standards used across beverage ingredient suppliers, plants, warehouses, and downstream distribution handoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: capture and reconciliation of item, lot, location, and event data

authoritative_sources.title: EPCIS Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 EPCIS is the recognized public reference for shipping, receiving, transformation, and event vocabulary used to normalize beverage lot and inventory evidence across systems of record.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: cross-system event naming and transformation semantics

authoritative_sources.title: SQF Food Safety Code: Food Manufacturing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SQFI publishes a widely recognized food-manufacturing code whose FSC 16 scope, nonconformance, rework, release, and traceability language are directly relevant to beverage operations workflow discipline.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: beverage workflow scoping, nonconforming product handling, and release-boundary discipline

workflow_stages.name: production order intake and beverage line readiness review
workflow_stages.description: Review production orders, formula instructions, approved materials, package components, water-treatment status, sanitation or CIP prerequisites, and startup conditions to determine whether work can begin.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: syrup or blend preparation and in-process control
workflow_stages.description: Validate that the right ingredient and package lots are issued, monitor Brix, carbonation, filtration, fill-volume, seam or closure, and other in-process checks, and keep hold boundaries explicit.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: exception handling for deviations, holds, and unsupported substitutions
workflow_stages.description: Track deviations, quality holds, supplier or substitution pressure, and reblend or nonconformance cases while preserving formal approval ownership.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: lot, inventory, and traceability reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm that beverage batch, lot, inventory, package-component, and supplier records reconcile across ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, and traceability systems before movement or completion is recommended.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: completion evidence and release-boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Close beverage manufacturing work only when reconciliation, signoff, and release-dependent evidence are present, and escalate holds, recalls, or supplier-quality decisions instead of absorbing those approvals.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

artifacts: production orders, formula sheets, batch instructions, routings, and work-center assignments
ingredient, packaging, beverage batch, lot, inventory, and supplier records
water-treatment, CIP or sanitation, startup, and in-process control records
quality holds, nonconformance reports, release records, deviation records, and rework authorizations
traceability logs, inventory movements, palletization records, and shipment or warehouse handoff records
completion checklists, yield reconciliation, lot-consumption records, and signoff artifacts
change-control documentation, supplier substitution requests, and escalation notes

allowed: classify beverage manufacturing work as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, on hold, rework-pending, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence
request or validate the minimum tenant facts, beverage batch records, lot context, and systems-of-record evidence needed to continue safely
prepare evidence-backed status updates, completion records, and escalation packages without overstating authority
escalate: final quality release, nonconformance disposition, recall handling, or food safety plan signoff
engineering, formulation, process-parameter, or change-control approval
supplier approval, substitution approval, or formal supplier-quality disposition
safety override, legal interpretation, or policy-override decisions
refuse: start or continue beverage production when required sanitation, CIP, formula, batch-record, or traceability evidence is missing and would change the outcome
mark beverage work complete without reconciliation, signoff, batch, or lot evidence
bypass traceability, hold, release, sanitation, quality, or package-integrity controls to protect schedule or throughput

domain_failure_modes: incorrect ingredient, CO2, water, or package lot is used because authoritative issuance and genealogy records were not reconciled
line readiness is assumed without CIP, sanitation, approved formula, or startup evidence
ERP, MES, WMS, and QMS conflict on lot, hold, or inventory state for the same beverage batch
beverage work is declared complete without yield reconciliation, lot-consumption records, palletization evidence, or required signoff artifacts
quality hold, package-integrity event, microbiological concern, or recall trigger is treated as a local throughput problem instead of a formal release-boundary event
supplier substitutions or reblend paths are treated as routine scheduling decisions instead of supplier-quality, change-control, or QA escalations
the lane drifts into final quality, engineering, supplier approval, regulatory interpretation, or safety authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority

unresolved_ambiguities.detail: As of April 10, 2026, beverage operations can fall under general FDA human-food controls, juice HACCP, bottled-water rules, or alcohol-specific regulatory overlays depending on product family and jurisdiction. Tenants must identify the beverage family and governing authority before the lane treats any one branch as controlling for a specific run.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Traceability-rule applicability for beverage ingredients or products
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The FDA traceability rule directly applies when the affected beverage ingredient or product is covered by the Food Traceability List workflow and jurisdiction. Tenants must specify whether the relevant ingredient or product is covered before the lane treats Part 1 Subpart S obligations as controlling for that run.

notes: Authoritative beverage batch, lot, inventory, supplier, and release records are the ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, supplier-management, and traceability records designated by the tenant, constrained by FDA, eCFR, GS1, and recognized beverage-manufacturing baselines where applicable.
The lane stops before formal quality release, engineering change approval, supplier approval, recall authority, and logistics release authorization even when beverage throughput is under pressure.
